Introduction Approach Geographies Deliverables Investment
Qualitative Research Proposal — May 2026

Exploring Veterinary Access Among Underserved Pet Owners: A Qualitative Look

A proposed qualitative addition to the PetSmart Charities-Gallup State of Pet Care Study — going deeper with low-income, rural, and remote pet owners in the United States and Canada whose experiences are not captured in the existing survey data.

🌎 US + Canada 👥 20 In-Depth Interviews 💻 Virtual & In-Person Options 📰 Media & Press Integration
1
Amplifying voices, filling a critical gap

PetSmart Charities-Gallup State of Pet Care Study tells a powerful national story about veterinary access barriers. But behind the numbers are pet owners whose lived experiences are even more acute — and whose voices add crucial depth to the story. This research is designed to bring in a broader range of pet parent perspectives, including low-income, rural, and remote owners in communities like Canada’s Northwest Territories who are rarely represented in national research.

It is an opportunity to shift from measuring the problem to humanizing it — putting real stories alongside the statistics, making the findings more compelling to the public and more actionable for program and policy design.

The communities we are proposing to include are already on PetSmart Charities’ radar. Colleagues working with local low-cost veterinary clinics and mobile vet programs in the field are encountering these pet owners regularly — people making impossible choices about their animals’ health. This research gives those encounters a structured, evidentiary foundation.

The opportunity

By incorporating the qualitative voices of underserved pet owners directly into the PetSmart Charities-Gallup State of Pet Care Study, PetSmart Charities can deepen the impact of the research, strengthen its advocacy position, and connect with audiences who see themselves — or their communities — reflected in the findings for the first time.

2
Our approach: community-partnered in-depth interviews

We propose 10 in-depth interviews (IDIs) per country — 20 interviews in total — with a purposively selected range of pet parents including low-income, rural, and remote owners who may not be captured through traditional survey sampling. In-depth interviews allow us to reach a broader cross-section of pet owners while generating the kind of rich, nuanced insight that surveys alone cannot provide.

Why IDIs? Qualitative research gives the numbers a human voice. The survey tells us that cost and distance are barriers. The IDI tells us what a pet owner actually does at 11pm when a dog is sick, there is no emergency vet within 90 miles, and the household has $40 until next payday. That is what makes findings actionable for program and policy design — and compelling to a general audience.

🤝 A collaborative recruitment model

We will work hand-in-hand with PetSmart Charities to engage their existing partner network — including low-cost veterinary clinics, mobile vet programs, and community animal welfare organizations — to identify and invite participants. This collaborative approach ensures we reach the full range of pet owners we want to hear from, including those who may not respond to traditional recruitment methods, and that participation feels trusted and authentic.

Interview format: virtual or in-person

We are proposing two delivery options, which can be combined depending on geography, participant access, and budget.

💻

Virtual interviews

Conducted via phone or video (WhatsApp, FaceTime, or similar). Best suited for participants with reliable connectivity, or where travel logistics are prohibitive.

  • · Lower cost per interview
  • · Wider geographic reach
  • · Easier scheduling flexibility
  • · Phone-only option for low-connectivity areas
👥

In-person interviews

Conducted at a community location (partner site, community centre, or participant’s home). Enables richer rapport and observational context.

  • · Deeper rapport and trust-building
  • · Stronger media & storytelling potential
  • · Enables photography / video for press use

Interview design

  • —Length: 45–60 minutes per interview, scheduled around participants’ availability
  • —Incentive: $150 USD / CAD equivalent per interview, in recognition of participants’ time and circumstances
  • —Discussion guide themes: Decision-making around veterinary care; barriers that lead to deferred or foregone treatment; informal workarounds and community resourcefulness; cost and other access challenges; and the emotional and practical challenges of caring for pets

Recruitment: community partners as the gateway

In close collaboration with PetSmart Charities, we will identify and engage community partner organizations per geography to serve as trusted liaisons for recruitment. Partners will include organizations already serving the target populations in their daily work.

Partner typeRole and rationale
Low-cost & mobile veterinary clinicsVet school outreach clinics, Humane Society mobile units, and community low-cost vet programs serve cost-constrained pet owners by definition. Staff are trusted liaisons already known to participants and ideally positioned to extend invitations to participate.
Community animal welfare organizationsLocal SPCAs, rescue organizations, and animal welfare nonprofits operating in target geographies provide established community presence and referral networks, particularly in remote Canadian communities.
Community health workers / promotorasEssential for US border region and rural communities. The established, evidence-based pathway to Spanish-dominant and other underserved households. Their trusted status removes institutional barriers to participation.
Friendship Centres & community service hubsEstablished service hubs in rural and remote Canadian communities with deep community trust — providing pathways to residents not reached by other means.
📰 Press & media participation: built in from day one

As part of the consent and recruitment process, we will invite willing participants to be available for press interviews, photo opportunities, or brief video features to support the public launch of the findings. This gives the report a human face, makes the research compelling to general audiences, and amplifies PetSmart Charities’ impact narrative. All participation in press activities will be fully voluntary and separately consented.

3
Proposed geographies

We propose conducting research in 2 locations in the United States and 2 locations in Canada’s Northwest Territories, selected to capture a range of pet owner experiences across different geographic and economic contexts. Sites will be finalized in collaboration with PetSmart Charities, informed by their existing partner footprint, geographic diversity, and the communities they most want to hear from.

Geography typeProfile and rationale
US Rural low-income communitye.g., Appalachian or Deep South region Persistently low-income rural areas with limited veterinary infrastructure, geographic isolation, and strong pet ownership culture. Human healthcare access barriers in these regions mirror veterinary access patterns exactly. Low-cost clinic and mobile vet program presence provides recruitment pathways.
US Rural community of colore.g., Mississippi Delta or similar Rural communities with majority populations of color who are systematically underrepresented in national research. Adds the critical dimension of racial and economic inequity to US findings, surfacing experiences not captured in nationally representative samples.
US Border / immigrant communitye.g., US-Mexico border region Low-income communities with language access barriers and limited formal service infrastructure. Pet ownership is culturally central; formal vet care is rare. Adds language as an explicit and intersecting barrier to access. Bilingual recruitment materials and interviewers required.
CA NWT regional hubYellowknife and surrounding area A regional centre that also serves as a gateway for smaller and more remote surrounding NWT communities. Provides access to a range of residents — from urban lower-income households to those who travel from outlying areas for services.
CA Remote NWT communitySmall community, fly-in or road-accessible Small, geographically remote NWT community where pet ownership — including working dogs — is part of daily life, and where formal veterinary access is extremely limited or entirely absent. Represents a northern experience fundamentally different from any southern sample.
CA Northern service communityNWT or comparable northern community A comparable remote northern context that can validate or contrast the regional hub findings, strengthening the analytical value of the Canadian component and providing geographic breadth to the cross-national analysis.

Final geography selection will be confirmed with PetSmart Charities. The profiles above represent the types of communities we recommend; specific locations will reflect partner presence and strategic fit.

4
What this research delivers

Qualitative findings will be woven directly into the public release of the PetSmart Charities-Gallup State of Pet Care Study, adding human stories and texture to the quantitative data.

👥
Recruitment support
Full recruitment design and execution in collaboration with PetSmart Charities’ partner network, including screener development, partner briefings, and participant coordination.
📄
Discussion guide
A co-developed, moderator-ready discussion guide tailored to each geography, translated where required.
🎥
Recordings & transcripts
Full audio or video recordings (with participant consent) and verbatim transcripts of all interviews, provided to PetSmart Charities.
📚
Findings in the public report
Qualitative themes, insights, and illustrative quotes woven directly into the public-facing PetSmart Charities-Gallup State of Pet Care Study alongside the quantitative findings.
📰
Quotes & media access
A curated bank of participant quotes for press use, plus direct access to willing participants for media interviews, features, and press materials at launch.
🌟
Human stories for launch
Participant profiles and storytelling assets (with consent) designed from the start to support a compelling, human-centered launch narrative for the report.
5
Investment

All-in pricing is provided below by delivery format. Each option covers the full scope of work: recruitment support, discussion guide development, moderation, recording, transcription, analysis, and integration of findings into the PetSmart Charities-Gallup State of Pet Care Study report. In-person pricing includes travel costs to 2 locations per country (4 sites total).

💻

Virtual

Phone or video interviews across all sites. Full scope including recruitment, moderation, transcription, analysis, and report integration.
$36,000
👥

In-person

On-site interviews at 4 locations. Full scope including recruitment, travel, moderation, transcription, analysis, and report integration.
$48,000
